title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 — Practice Set 3: Counting & Logic
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many stars are shown in the picture?
questions.image_description: A row of five identical stars.
questions.solution: Count the stars one by one: there are five stars in the row.

questions.question_text: Lena has 3 red apples and 4 green apples. How many apples does she have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' width='300' height='80' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor'><circle cx='25' cy='40' r='14' fill='#f0f0f0'/><circle cx='60' cy='40' r='14' fill='#f0f0f0'/><circle cx='95' cy='40' r='14' fill='#f0f0f0'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='14' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='185' cy='40' r='14' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='220' cy='40' r='14' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='255' cy='40' r='14' fill='#e8e8e8'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three lighter apples on the left and four darker apples on the right.
questions.solution: Add 3 and 4 to get 7 apples total.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in the figure (small and large)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 120' width='200' height='120' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><polygon points='100,10 40,100 160,100'/><line x1='100' y1='10' x2='100' y2='100'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle split by a vertical line from the top vertex to the base.
questions.solution: Two small triangles on the left and right, plus the one large triangle, gives 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: Tom has 8 candies. He gives 3 to his sister. How many candies does Tom have left?
questions.solution: Subtract: 8 minus 3 equals 5 candies.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly 4 sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' width='300' height='80' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='20'/><polygon points='90,20 115,60 65,60'/><polygon points='160,25 185,25 195,45 172,60 150,45'/><rect x='220' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='285,20 300,32 295,52 275,52 270,32'/></g><g font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'><text x='30' y='78'>A</text><text x='90' y='78'>B</text><text x='172' y='78'>C</text><text x='240' y='78'>D</text><text x='285' y='78'>E</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes labeled A through E: circle, triangle, pentagon, square, pentagon.
questions.solution: A square has exactly 4 sides, which is shape D.

questions.question_text: Which number comes next: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time, so the next number is 10.

questions.question_text: How many circles can you count in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 100' width='300' height='100' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='12'/><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='12'/><circle cx='110' cy='70' r='12'/><circle cx='150' cy='70' r='12'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Two rows of four circles each.
questions.solution: Two rows of 4 circles: 2 times 4 equals 8 circles.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are in the grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' width='120' height='80' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 2 by 3 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: Two rows of 3 gives 6 small squares.

questions.question_text: How many different paths go from A to B by moving only right or down along the lines?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 120' width='160' height='120' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor'><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='140' y2='20'/><line x1='20' y1='70' x2='140' y2='70'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='20' y2='70'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='70'/><line x1='140' y1='20' x2='140' y2='70'/></g><g font-size='14'><text x='5' y='18'>A</text><text x='145' y='75'>B</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A grid of 2 columns and 1 row from A at top-left to B at bottom-right.
questions.solution: The monotone paths on a 2 by 1 grid equal C(3,1)=3: right-right-down, right-down-right, down-right-right.

questions.question_text: Anna, Ben, and Cara are standing in a line. Anna is in front of Ben. Cara is behind Ben. Who is last in line?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 80' width='240' height='80' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='18'/><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='18'/><circle cx='200' cy='40' r='18'/></g><g font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'><text x='40' y='70'>front</text><text x='200' y='70'>back</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circles in a row representing people lined up from front to back.
questions.solution: The order from front to back is Anna, Ben, Cara, so Cara is last.

questions.question_text: How many unit squares make up the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 140' width='140' height='140' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A staircase figure made of unit squares.
questions.solution: Count each unit square in the staircase: there are 7.

questions.question_text: A box holds 5 pencils. How many pencils are in 4 boxes?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 70' width='260' height='70' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='10' y='15' width='50' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='50' height='40'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='50' height='40'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='50' height='40'/></g><g font-size='14' text-anchor='middle'><text x='35' y='40'>5</text><text x='95' y='40'>5</text><text x='155' y='40'>5</text><text x='215' y='40'>5</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four boxes each labeled with a 5.
questions.solution: Multiply: 4 times 5 equals 20 pencils.

questions.question_text: Three friends each have one of these pets: a cat, a dog, or a fish. Sam's pet has no fur. Tim's pet is not a cat. What pet does Sam have?
questions.solution: Among the three pets only the fish has no fur, so Sam has the fish.

questions.question_text: Which square piece completes the L-shape into a full rectangle of the matching size?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 110' width='300' height='110' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><polygon points='10,10 60,10 60,35 35,35 35,60 10,60'/><rect x='85' y='20' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='120' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='160' y='20' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='205' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='255' y='10' width='35' height='35'/></g><g font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'><text x='92' y='60'>A</text><text x='130' y='60'>B</text><text x='172' y='60'>C</text><text x='220' y='60'>D</text><text x='272' y='60'>E</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shape missing a 25 by 25 corner, followed by five squares of sizes 15, 20, 25, 30, and 35 labeled A-E.
questions.solution: The L-shape is missing a 25 by 25 corner, matching piece C.

questions.question_text: How many different 2-letter codes can you make using letters from {A, B, C} if letters may repeat?
questions.solution: Each of the 2 positions has 3 choices, so 3 times 3 equals 9 codes.

questions.question_text: Which figure below has the most corners (vertices)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' width='300' height='80' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><polygon points='30,15 50,55 10,55'/><rect x='75' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='160,15 185,15 195,35 172,55 150,35'/><polygon points='225,15 250,20 255,40 240,55 220,50 215,30'/><circle cx='285' cy='35' r='20'/></g><g font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'><text x='30' y='72'>A</text><text x='95' y='72'>B</text><text x='172' y='72'>C</text><text x='235' y='72'>D</text><text x='285' y='72'>E</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle, square, pentagon, hexagon, and circle labeled A-E.
questions.solution: The hexagon has 6 corners, more than the triangle (3), square (4), pentagon (5), or circle (0).

questions.question_text: How many different paths lead from A to B on a 2 by 2 grid, moving only right or down along the lines?
questions.image_description: A 2 by 2 grid with A at the top-left corner and B at the bottom-right corner.
questions.solution: The number of monotone paths equals C(4,2) = 6.

questions.question_text: Four kids sit in a row: Kai, Leo, Mia, and Nia. Mia is not at either end. Leo is to the right of Mia. Nia is to the left of Mia. Where is Kai?
questions.solution: The middle is fixed as Nia-Mia-Leo, so Kai sits at one of the two ends: far left or far right.

questions.question_text: A 4 by 4 square is missing its inner 2 by 2 center. How many unit squares must be added to complete the big square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 160' width='160' height='160' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='20' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='50' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='50' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='80' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='80' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='110' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='110' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='80' y='110' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='110' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 4 by 4 square frame with the inner 2 by 2 center empty.
questions.solution: The missing inner 2 by 2 region is 4 unit squares.

questions.question_text: A basket has 12 fruits. One third are apples, and the rest are pears. How many pears are there?
questions.solution: One third of 12 is 4 apples, so 12 minus 4 equals 8 pears.

questions.question_text: How many rectangles of any size can be found in a 1 by 3 strip of unit squares?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' width='200' height='60' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='10' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='40' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three unit squares in a row forming a 1 by 3 strip.
questions.solution: There are 3 single squares plus 2 double rectangles plus 1 triple rectangle, giving 6.

questions.question_text: Dan is 3 years older than Eva. In 2 years, Dan will be 10. How old is Eva now?
questions.solution: Dan is currently 10 minus 2 equals 8. Eva is 3 years younger, so Eva is 5.

questions.question_text: A bag has 2 red balls, 2 blue balls, and 1 green ball. What is the smallest number of balls you must take out (without looking) to be sure you have 2 balls of the same color?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' width='200' height='60' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='12' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='125' cy='30' r='12' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='12' fill='#f0f0f0'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five balls: two red, two blue, and one green.
questions.solution: With 3 balls you might get one of each color, but with 4 balls two must share a color since there are only 3 colors.

questions.question_text: Three boxes are labeled Apples, Pears, and Mixed. Every label is wrong. You may pick one fruit from one box to figure out all three. Which box should you pick from?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 90' width='300' height='90' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'><rect x='20' y='20' width='70' height='50'/><rect x='115' y='20' width='70' height='50'/><rect x='210' y='20' width='70' height='50'/></g><g font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'><text x='55' y='50'>Apples</text><text x='150' y='50'>Pears</text><text x='245' y='50'>Mixed</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three boxes labeled Apples, Pears, and Mixed, all labels wrong.
questions.solution: Because all labels are wrong, the Mixed box must hold only one fruit type. That single pick identifies it, and the other two boxes are forced by elimination.
